ddls:
CREATE TABLE "components" (
  "part_number" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the electronic component */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the component */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the component */,
  "notice_date" TEXT /* Date when the notice of obsolescence was issued */,
  "ltb_date" TEXT /* Last time buy date for the component */,
  "ship_date" TEXT /* Date after which the component is no longer shipped */,
  "replacement_part" TEXT /* Part number of the recommended replacement component */,
  "compliance_standard" TEXT /* Compliance standard the component adheres to */,
  "product_category" TEXT /* Product category the component belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("part_number")
)

CREATE TABLE "component_notices" (
  "notice_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notice */,
  "part_number" TEXT /* Part number of the component the notice is for */,
  "notice_description" TEXT /* Description of the notice */,
  "issue_date" TEXT /* Date when the notice was issued */,
  "notice_type" TEXT /* Type of notice (e.g., obsolescence, discontinuation) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notice_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_component_notices_part_number FOREIGN KEY ("part_number") REFERENCES components ("part_number")
)

CREATE TABLE "replacement_parts" (
  "replacement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each replacement part */,
  "part_number" TEXT /* Part number of the obsolete component */,
  "replacement_part_number" TEXT /* Part number of the replacement component */,
  "compatibility_notes" TEXT /* Notes on compatibility issues with the replacement part */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("replacement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_replacement_parts_part_number FOREIGN KEY ("part_number") REFERENCES components ("part_number")
)

CREATE TABLE "inventory" (
  "inventory_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each inventory record */,
  "part_number" TEXT /* Part number of the component */,
  "quantity_on_hand" INTEGER /* Quantity of the component currently in stock */,
  "warehouse_location" TEXT /* Location of the component in the warehouse */,
  "storage_conditions" TEXT /* Storage conditions for the component (e.g., temperature, humidity) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("inventory_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventory_part_number FOREIGN KEY ("part_number") REFERENCES components ("part_number")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, procurement, manager) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "part_number" TEXT /* Part number of the component being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the component data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the component data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, update) */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments or notes about the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_part_number FOREIGN KEY ("part_number") REFERENCES components ("part_number"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "component_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each change event */,
  "part_number" TEXT /* Part number of the component being changed */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was made */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Description of the change made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_component_history_part_number FOREIGN KEY ("part_number") REFERENCES components ("part_number")
)

CREATE TABLE "erp_integration" (
  "integration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each integration event */,
  "erp_system" TEXT /* ERP system being integrated with */,
  "integration_date" TEXT /* Date when the integration was performed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the integration (e.g., success, failure) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("integration_id")
)
